use_case: Vehicle recall root cause analysis and defect pattern identification
space_title: Apex Motor Group - Recall & Defect Analytics 🚨
space_description: Recall and defect analytics for Apex Motor Group. Ask questions about recall events, warranty claims, component failure patterns, root cause categories, and repair outcomes across 20 vehicle models and 15 suppliers.

tables.description: Recall event records capturing NHTSA-filed recalls linked to vehicle models, with severity, root cause, supplier, remedy, and cost data.

columns.name: recall_event_id
columns.comment: Unique recall event identifier

columns.name: vehicle_model_id
columns.comment: Vehicle model identifier

columns.name: make
columns.comment: Vehicle manufacturer brand

columns.name: model
columns.comment: Vehicle model name

columns.name: model_year
columns.comment: Vehicle model year

columns.name: vehicle_class
columns.comment: Vehicle classification

columns.name: recall_date
columns.comment: Date the recall was filed

columns.name: recall_nhtsa_number
columns.comment: NHTSA recall campaign number

columns.name: component_group
columns.comment: Component system affected by the recall
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.071 THEN 'Infotainment System' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.143 THEN 'Visibility/Wipers' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.214 THEN 'Fuel System' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.286 THEN 'Powertrain' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.357 THEN 'Brakes' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.429 THEN 'Suspension' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.500 THEN 'Exterior Lighting' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.571 THEN 'Wheels/Tires' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.643 THEN 'Steering' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.714 THEN 'Seats/Restraints' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.786 THEN 'Electrical System' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.857 THEN 'ADAS Software' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.929 THEN 'Engine Cooling' ELSE 'Air Bags' END

columns.name: defect_description
columns.comment: Free-text description of the defect
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.071 THEN 'Touchscreen may become unresponsive during operation' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.143 THEN 'Wiper motor may fail intermittently in heavy rain conditions' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.214 THEN 'Fuel line connector may develop micro-cracks causing vapor leak' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.286 THEN 'Transmission control module may cause unexpected downshift' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.357 THEN 'Brake caliper mounting bolt may loosen over time' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.429 THEN 'Front strut bearing may corrode prematurely in salt belt regions' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.500 THEN 'Daytime running light LED module may overheat and fail' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.571 THEN 'Wheel lug nuts may not maintain proper torque specification' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.643 THEN 'Power steering assist may reduce unexpectedly at low speed' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.714 THEN 'Seat belt pretensioner may not deploy in rear-end collision' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.786 THEN 'Battery management system may incorrectly report state of charge' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.857 THEN 'Forward collision warning may generate false alerts in fog' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.929 THEN 'Coolant thermostat housing may crack causing slow coolant loss' ELSE 'Side curtain airbag inflator may produce excessive force' END
entity_values: Touchscreen may become unresponsive during operation
Wiper motor may fail intermittently in heavy rain conditions
Fuel line connector may develop micro-cracks causing vapor leak
Transmission control module may cause unexpected downshift
Brake caliper mounting bolt may loosen over time
Front strut bearing may corrode prematurely in salt belt regions
Daytime running light LED module may overheat and fail
Wheel lug nuts may not maintain proper torque specification
Power steering assist may reduce unexpectedly at low speed
Seat belt pretensioner may not deploy in rear-end collision
Battery management system may incorrectly report state of charge
Forward collision warning may generate false alerts in fog
Coolant thermostat housing may crack causing slow coolant loss
Side curtain airbag inflator may produce excessive force

columns.name: severity_level
columns.comment: Recall severity classification
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.44 THEN 'Minor' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.74 THEN 'Major' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.91 THEN 'Safety Advisory' ELSE 'Critical' END

columns.name: units_affected
columns.comment: Number of vehicles affected by the recall

columns.name: estimated_repair_cost_per_unit
columns.comment: Estimated repair cost per vehicle in USD

columns.name: total_estimated_cost
columns.comment: Total estimated financial impact of the recall in USD

columns.name: root_cause_category
columns.comment: Root cause classification for the defect
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {seed} % 100 < 11 THEN 'Calibration Drift' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 22 THEN 'Supplier Quality Escape' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 33 THEN 'Assembly Error' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 44 THEN 'Inadequate Testing' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 55 THEN 'Environmental Degradation' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 65 THEN 'Design Deficiency' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 75 THEN 'Manufacturing Process Error' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 84 THEN 'Material Specification Failure' WHEN {seed} % 100 < 93 THEN 'Component Wear-Out' ELSE 'Software Bug' END

columns.name: supplier_id
columns.comment: Tier-1 supplier identifier
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {seed} % 15 = 0 THEN 'SUP-BOSCH' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 1 THEN 'SUP-DENSO' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 2 THEN 'SUP-MAGNA' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 3 THEN 'SUP-CONTI' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 4 THEN 'SUP-ZF' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 5 THEN 'SUP-AISIN' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 6 THEN 'SUP-LEAR' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 7 THEN 'SUP-APTIV' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 8 THEN 'SUP-VALEO' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 9 THEN 'SUP-HELLA' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 10 THEN 'SUP-YAZAKI' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 11 THEN 'SUP-SUMITOMO' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 12 THEN 'SUP-MANDO' WHEN {seed} % 15 = 13 THEN 'SUP-SCHAEFFLER' ELSE 'SUP-NEXTEER' END

columns.name: days_to_remedy
columns.comment: Number of days from recall announcement to remedy availability

columns.name: remedy_type
columns.comment: Type of remedy applied to resolve the recall
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.31 THEN 'Repair' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.55 THEN 'Replace' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.78 THEN 'Software Update' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.91 THEN 'Inspection' ELSE 'Owner Notification' END

columns.name: injuries_reported
columns.comment: Number of injuries reported related to this recall
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.70 THEN 0 WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.85 THEN 1 WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.92 THEN CAST(2 + FLOOR({status_noise} * 3) AS INT) WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.97 THEN CAST(5 + FLOOR({status_noise} * 5) AS INT) ELSE CAST(10 + FLOOR({status_noise} * 5) AS INT) END

columns.name: fatalities_reported
columns.comment: Number of fatalities reported related to this recall
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.985 THEN 0 WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.995 THEN 1 WHEN {qty_noise} < 0.999 THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

columns.name: status
columns.comment: Current status of the recall campaign
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.57 THEN 'Closed' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.85 THEN 'In Progress' ELSE 'Open' END

tables.description: Warranty claim records from dealerships, capturing repair costs, labor, component codes, and claim disposition for vehicles under warranty coverage.

columns.name: claim_id
columns.comment: Unique warranty claim identifier

columns.name: vehicle_model_id
columns.comment: Vehicle model identifier

columns.name: make
columns.comment: Vehicle manufacturer brand

columns.name: model
columns.comment: Vehicle model name

columns.name: model_year
columns.comment: Vehicle model year

columns.name: vehicle_class
columns.comment: Vehicle classification

columns.name: claim_date
columns.comment: Date the warranty claim was filed

columns.name: mileage_at_claim
columns.comment: Vehicle odometer reading at time of claim

columns.name: component_code
columns.comment: Component code identifying the failed part
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {seed} % 30 = 0 THEN 'SU-BLJ' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 1 THEN 'PT-TBW' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 2 THEN 'BR-PAD' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 3 THEN 'EL-BCM' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 4 THEN 'EN-THR' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 5 THEN 'IN-TCH' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 6 THEN 'FS-INJ' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 7 THEN 'ST-RCK' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 8 THEN 'CL-RAD' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 9 THEN 'EX-LED' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 10 THEN 'SE-BKL' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 11 THEN 'WH-BRG' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 12 THEN 'AD-CAM' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 13 THEN 'AB-SNS' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 14 THEN 'PT-CVT' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 15 THEN 'BR-ABS' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 16 THEN 'EL-ALT' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 17 THEN 'EN-WTP' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 18 THEN 'SU-SWA' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 19 THEN 'IN-AMP' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 20 THEN 'FS-PMP' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 21 THEN 'ST-EPS' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 22 THEN 'CL-FAN' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 23 THEN 'EX-HRN' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 24 THEN 'SE-MTR' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 25 THEN 'WH-TPS' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 26 THEN 'AD-RDR' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 27 THEN 'AB-INF' WHEN {seed} % 30 = 28 THEN 'PT-OIL' ELSE 'BR-ROT' END

columns.name: labor_hours
columns.comment: Total labor hours charged for the repair

columns.name: parts_cost_usd
columns.comment: Cost of replacement parts in USD

columns.name: labor_cost_usd
columns.comment: Labor cost in USD

columns.name: total_claim_cost_usd
columns.comment: Total warranty claim cost (parts + labor)

columns.name: region
columns.comment: Geographic region where the claim was filed
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.17 THEN 'Northwest' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.33 THEN 'Midwest' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.48 THEN 'Mountain' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.62 THEN 'Southwest' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.76 THEN 'Southeast' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.90 THEN 'West Coast' ELSE 'Northeast' END

columns.name: claim_disposition
columns.comment: Claim review outcome
columns.generation_expr: CASE WHEN {status_noise} < 0.62 THEN 'Approved' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.77 THEN 'Partially Approved' WHEN {status_noise} < 0.90 THEN 'Pending Review' ELSE 'Denied' END

tables.description: Monthly aggregate recall KPIs tracking recall volume, closure rates, cost, remedy timelines, and customer satisfaction scores.

columns.name: month_date
columns.comment: First day of the reporting month

columns.name: metric_period
columns.comment: Reporting period granularity

columns.name: total_recalls_opened
columns.comment: Number of new recalls opened in the month

columns.name: total_recalls_closed
columns.comment: Number of recalls closed in the month

columns.name: units_under_recall
columns.comment: Total vehicle units with active recall status

columns.name: avg_days_to_remedy
columns.comment: Average days from recall to remedy availability

columns.name: total_cost_usd
columns.comment: Total recall cost for the month in USD

columns.name: customer_satisfaction_score
columns.comment: Monthly customer satisfaction score (0-100 scale)

measures.name: critical_recall_count
measures.expr: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN severity_level = 'Critical' THEN recall_event_id END)

measures.name: total_estimated_repair_cost_per_unit
measures.comment: Total estimated repair cost per unit

measures.name: open_recall_event_count
measures.expr: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN status = 'Open' THEN recall_event_id END)

genie_instructions:
You are an automotive analytics assistant for Apex Motor Group. Answer questions about vehicle recall root cause analysis and defect pattern identification using the tables and metric views below.

- When a question aggregates a measure (totals, counts, averages, rates), use the metric views with MEASURE() and GROUP BY ALL.
- When a question needs a base-table column (vehicle_model_id, make, model, model_year), query the base tables directly.
- When joining tables, use the shared keys: vehicle_model_id (joins recall_events and warranty_claims).
- When the user's phrasing matches a SQL snippet display name (filters, expressions, or measures), prefer that snippet.
- Treat the `status` field as authoritative for lifecycle state.
sample_questions: What is the critical recall count by component group?
Which component groups have the highest open status count?
How does the number of unique vehicle models vary by component group?
How many unique suppliers are there per component group?
Which regions have the most unique vehicle models?
How does the number of unique components vary by region?
What is the highest mileage at claim for each region?

example_sqls.question: How does the number of unique vehicle models vary by component group?
How many unique suppliers are there per component group?
What is the highest mileage at claim for each region?
How does total number of vehicles affected by the recall break down by vehicle model for 'Closed' records?
sql_lines: SELECT vehicle_model_id, COUNT(*) AS record_count, SUM(units_affected) AS total_units_affected
WHERE status = 'Closed' GROUP BY vehicle_model_id ORDER BY total_units_affected DESC

example_sqls.question: What is the trend of total vehicle odometer reading at time of claim over time?
sql_lines: GROUP BY claim_date ORDER BY claim_date

example_sqls.question: Show the distribution of records by reporting period
sql_lines: GROUP BY metric_period ORDER BY record_count DESC

example_sqls.question: Which component groups have the most unique suppliers?

filters.instruction: Use when the user asks about critical severity only.
Use when the user asks about current year recalls.
Use when the user asks about high cost recalls.

expressions.sql: CASE WHEN severity_level IN ('Critical', 'Safety Advisory') THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END

benchmarks.question: What is the critical recall count by component group?
Which component groups have the highest open status count?
Which regions have the most unique vehicle models?
How does the number of unique components vary by region?
What are the top vehicle model by total number of vehicles affected by the recall?
sql_lines: GROUP BY vehicle_model_id ORDER BY total_units_affected DESC LIMIT 10

benchmarks.question: How many records are there per date the warranty claim was filed?
sql_lines: GROUP BY claim_date ORDER BY claim_date

benchmarks.question: Which reporting period have the most records?
sql_lines: GROUP BY metric_period ORDER BY record_count DESC LIMIT 10
